subject: Mr Friess Course Samples
topic: Careers 8, literacy, and Long Way Down classroom materials

careers8_personality_core_notes.metadata_topic: Personality, science, and the limits of labels
careers8_personality_core_notes.title: Careers 8 Guided Notes
careers8_personality_core_notes.subtitle: MBTI, Big Five, and the limits of labels
careers8_personality_core_notes.learning_target: I can explain one difference between MBTI and the Big Five.
careers8_personality_core_notes.directions: Use the left column for key questions. Use the right column for lesson notes, examples, and reminders.
careers8_personality_core_notes.summary_prompt: What is the most important idea from today's lesson?

careers8_personality_extension.metadata_topic: Personality, science, and the limits of labels
careers8_personality_extension.title: Careers 8 Extension Notes
careers8_personality_extension.subtitle: Apply the Barnum Effect and critique personality labels
careers8_personality_extension.learning_target: I can evaluate how personality labels can help and limit people.
careers8_personality_extension.directions: Go beyond copying notes. Add examples, connections, and a clear claim about personality testing.
prompts: How does a vague description feel personal?
Where do we see this outside class?
Where does the label break down?
Should employers use this kind of test?
success_criteria: I used at least two lesson details.
I added my own example or critique.
My claim is clear and supported.

careers8_personality_compare.title: MBTI vs. Big Five Organizer
careers8_personality_compare.subtitle: Compare the models before judging usefulness
careers8_personality_compare.prompt: Compare MBTI and the Big Five. Focus on evidence, reliability, and how each model describes people.
success_criteria: I included specific details, not vague similarities.
I wrote at least one genuine overlap.
My final judgment uses evidence from the comparison.

careers8_personality_exit.title: Careers 8 Exit Ticket
careers8_personality_exit.subtitle: MBTI and Big Five exit check
careers8_personality_exit.learning_target: I can explain one key difference between MBTI and the Big Five.
careers8_personality_exit.prompt: In your own words, what is one key difference between MBTI and the Big Five?
success_criteria: I answered in my own words.
I named one clear difference.
I used accurate vocabulary from the lesson.

long_way_down_floor_notes.title: Long Way Down Encounter Notes
long_way_down_floor_notes.subtitle: What is revealed and how Will is affected
long_way_down_floor_notes.learning_target: I can track how an encounter changes pressure, memory, or choice.
long_way_down_floor_notes.directions: Use page references or short evidence notes. Do not copy long passages from the novel.
long_way_down_floor_notes.summary_prompt: How did this encounter change your understanding of Will?

long_way_down_theme_tracker.title: Long Way Down Theme Tracker
long_way_down_theme_tracker.subtitle: Rules, grief, revenge, and choice
long_way_down_theme_tracker.prompt: Track one theme idea across several moments. Use page references or short evidence notes instead of copied passages.
columns: What the novel may be suggesting
success_criteria: My theme idea is not just a plot event.
My evidence points to a specific moment.
My explanation connects the moment to a bigger idea.

long_way_down_cer.title: Long Way Down Theme CER
long_way_down_cer.subtitle: Build a claim about rules, grief, revenge, or choice
long_way_down_cer.question: What is the novel suggesting about rules, grief, revenge, or choice?
long_way_down_cer.learning_target: I can build a theme claim and support it with evidence.
long_way_down_cer.directions: Choose one theme idea. Make a claim, point to a specific moment, and explain how that moment supports your thinking.
sentence_starters: One moment that supports this is...
The reader is invited to question...
success_criteria: My claim is about a theme.
My evidence points to a specific moment.
My reasoning explains the connection.
I avoided copying long passages.

long_way_down_exit.title: Long Way Down Exit Reflection
long_way_down_exit.subtitle: Question, detail, and next reading move
long_way_down_exit.learning_target: I can reflect on how today's reading changed my thinking.
long_way_down_exit.prompt: What question are you carrying into the next section? Which detail created that question?
success_criteria: I wrote one genuine question.
I named the detail or structure that created it.
I explained why the question matters.

literacy_prefix_word_study.title: Word Study: Prefix + Root
literacy_prefix_word_study.subtitle: Break the word before guessing the meaning
literacy_prefix_word_study.directions: Choose four words. Circle the prefix, underline the root, and use both parts to predict the meaning.
literacy_prefix_word_study.prompt: Focus prefixes: re-, pre-, un-. Explain how the prefix changes the meaning of the root word.
success_criteria: I marked the prefix and root.
I predicted the meaning before checking.
I used one word in a clear sentence.

literacy_comprehension_ladder.title: Reading Thinking Ladder
literacy_comprehension_ladder.subtitle: Notice, wonder, connect, and explain
literacy_comprehension_ladder.prompt: Use this after a short reading, article, or class excerpt. Move from noticing to explaining your thinking.
success_criteria: I wrote at least one specific notice.
I asked a real question.
I explained one connection or idea.
